title: Review: wolftpm crates (standalone + TSS)
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Two P2 issues: FMT1 code resolution affects any user who reads error messages from TPM; optional-source skip could produce linker errors on unusual wolfTPM versions.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: the FMT1 resolution issue (xsmt.1/xsmt.2) — I'm confident about the diagnostic impact but not certain how often wolfTPM actually returns FMT1-qualified codes through the paths used here vs. base codes only. Uncertainty driven by: no runtime access to test with a real TPM; wolfTPM's actual return-code behavior for error paths in wolfTPM2_VerifyHashScheme not verified against source. Would raise confidence if: could run against a real or software TPM and inspect returned error codes.
Idiom rating: CLEAN — no idiomatic Rust issues found. Ownership, error handling, lifetimes, unsafe, and trait impls are all correct and idiomatic. Rust idiom confidence: 10% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: none. Uncertainty driven by: did not run clippy; could not observe actual wolfTPM return codes at runtime.
